name: The ascent San Felices from Aguilar del Río Alhama — circular by al Castillo de Magaña de Los
name_original: La subida San Felices desde Aguilar del Río Alhama — circular por al Castillo de Magaña  de Los
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Aguilar del Río Alhama traverse the low-altitude mountainous foothills of the Iberian System in Spain's La Rioja region. The terrain features a mix of hills, valleys, vineyards, and rocky crags, shaped by the Alhama River. Cyclists can expect varied landscapes, from holm oak forests on Monegro Mountain to open views of the Val valley from Peña del Tío Fresquito. The area offers diverse routes suitable for road cycling, incorporating both natural beauty and historical landmarks. Best road cycling routes around Aguilar Del Río Alhama The most popular road cycling route is The ascent San Felices from Aguilar del Río Alhama — circular by al Castillo de Magaña de Los, a 36.5 miles (58.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 8 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through the region's varied topography. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Cigudosa and Aguilar del Río Alhama Circular, a moderate 21.1 miles (34.0 km) path. This route offers a balanced challenge with views of the surrounding hills and valleys. Local road cyclists also love the Navajun from Valdemadera — circular by the views to Castillo de Magaña and Cigudosa, a 35.9 miles (57.8 km) trail leading through varied landscapes with views towards historical sites, often completed in about 3 hours 10 minutes. Road cycling around Aguilar del Río Alhama is defined by rolling hills, river valleys, and historical sites like the Celtiberian archaeological site of Contrebia Leucade. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate climbs to more difficult ascents. The routes in Aguilar del Río Alhama are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 150 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Aguilar del Río Alhama's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Aguilar del Río Alhama?
A: There are over 10 road cycling routes available around Aguilar del Río Alhama. These routes offer a mix of challenges, with 5 moderate and 8 difficult options to explore the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Aguilar del Río Alhama?
A: The Mediterranean continental climate makes the warm summers, typically from June to September, ideal for road cycling. During these months, you can fully enjoy the varied terrain and scenic views.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Aguilar del Río Alhama?
A: You can expect a diverse landscape featuring rolling hills, picturesque valleys, vineyards, and rocky crags. Routes often follow the Alhama River, pass through holm oak forests on Monegro Mountain, and offer open views from spots like Peña del Tío Fresquito.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or less experienced riders?
A: While there are no routes specifically graded as 'easy', there are 5 moderate road cycling routes that offer a balanced challenge. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383520369/cigudosa-and-aguilar-del-rio-alhama-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cigudosa and Aguilar del Río Alhama Circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is 21.1 miles (34.0 km) long with a moderate elevation gain.

Q: What historical sites can I visit while road cycling around Aguilar del Río Alhama?
A: You can incorporate several historical sites into your rides. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5338604" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Celtiberian Archaeological Site of Contrebia Leucade&lt;/a&gt; is a significant and well-preserved ancient settlement. Additionally, the ruins of the Castle of Inestrillas offer natural viewpoints, and the Romanesque Hermitage of Santa María de La Antigua (or La Valvanera) is another notable historical stop.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, there are 8 difficult routes for experienced cyclists. A challenging option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383526284/the-ascent-san-felices-from-aguilar-del-rio-alhama-circular-by-al-castillo-de-magana-de-los" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The ascent San Felices from Aguilar del Río Alhama — circular by al Castillo de Magaña de Los&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 36.5 miles (58.7 km) with significant elevation gain.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: The region offers several natural attractions. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5573920" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Tío Fresquito&lt;/a&gt; for excellent views of the Val valley, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de las Truchas Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5575390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Val Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The Sierra del Tormo is also home to a significant colony of griffon vultures, offering wildlife observation opportunities.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383520369/cigudosa-and-aguilar-del-rio-alhama-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cigudosa and Aguilar del Río Alhama Circular&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383511516/navajun-from-valdemadera-circular-by-the-views-to-castillo-de-magana-and-cigudosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navajun from Valdemadera — circular by the views to Castillo de Magaña and Cigudosa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Aguilar del Río Alhama?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, which combines rolling hills and river valleys with historical sites, providing a rich and engaging experience.

Q: Are there any paved cycling trails suitable for road bikes?
A: The region is noted for its cycling infrastructure, including paths and bike lanes designed to avoid vehicular traffic, particularly around the Alhama River. Many road cycling routes utilize these paved sections, offering smooth surfaces for your ride.

Q: Can I find cafes or restaurants along the road cycling routes?
A: While specific cafes on routes are not detailed, Aguilar del Río Alhama and surrounding villages offer amenities where you can find places to eat and drink. Planning your route to pass through these towns will allow for refreshment stops.

Q: Is public transport available to Aguilar del Río Alhama for cyclists with bikes?
A: Information on specific public transport options for bikes is not readily available. It is advisable to check with local bus or train operators in the La Rioja region regarding their policies for transporting bicycles before planning your trip.

Q: Where can I find parking facilities for road cycling in Aguilar del Río Alhama?
A: Parking facilities are generally available within Aguilar del Río Alhama itself. For specific routes, it's recommended to check the starting points for designated parking areas, often found near town centers or popular trailheads.
tips: The road that connects the towns of San Felices with Aguilar del Río Alhama takes you along a narrow road that winds before descending to the second town mentioned.
A really nice training climb. With 4.48km and an average slope of 5.5%, you gain 253km to reach the summit at 903 meters above sea level in San Felices, a crossroads between La Rioja and Soria.

Before reaching the town of Magaña, in one of the curves that descends to the Río Alhama, you can see the Castillo de Magaña and the whole of its town located at one of the meanders of the same river.

waypoints.name: Views of the Castillo de Magaña
